## FHIR Queries — Neonatal Abstinence Syndrome (NAS/NOWS) Cohort

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|206521007,http://snomed.info/sct|722927005,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|P96.1

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|7052
```

---

### Code Rationale

#### Query 1 — Condition (Diagnosis-based arm)

| System | Code | Display |
|---|---|---|
| `http://snomed.info/sct` | `206521007` | Drug withdrawal syndrome in neonate |
| `http://snomed.info/sct` | `722927005` | Neonatal opioid withdrawal syndrome (NOWS) — more specific, added to reflect opioid-specific framing |
| `http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm` | `P96.1` | Neonatal withdrawal symptoms from maternal use of drugs of addiction |

> **Why both SNOMED and ICD-10-CM?** Clinical EHRs encode diagnoses heterogeneously. Legacy billing systems emit ICD-10-CM `P96.1`; FHIR-native and clinical decision support systems prefer SNOMED. Using a comma-separated `code=` list performs an **OR** across all three codes in a single round-trip.

#### Query 2 — MedicationRequest (Pharmacologic treatment arm)

| System | Code | Display |
|---|---|---|
| `http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm` | `7052` | Morphine (ingredient-level) |

> **Why ingredient-level RxNorm `7052`?** NAS/NOWS morphine tapers are dispensed as oral morphine sulfate solution in varying concentrations (e.g., 0.4 mg/mL, 2 mg/mL). Using the ingredient code subsumes all product-level and clinical drug RxNorm descendants, avoiding missed matches due to formulation-specific coding.

---

### Union Logic & Known Limitations

| Consideration | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Union** | Collect `subject` references from both result sets; deduplicate by `Patient.id` |
| **Sensitivity** | Each query alone is incomplete — diagnosis codes may lag clinical recognition; morphine may be prescribed for non-NAS indications in neonates |
| **Specificity guard** | In practice, restrict MedicationRequest arm to patients with `Patient.birthDate` within the last 28 days (neonatal window) via a chained `patient.birthdate=ge<date>` filter |
| **Out-of-scope (PheKB)** | Full PheKB NAS phenotype uses 6 opioid-exposure indicators (maternal opioid Rx, maternal OUD diagnosis, urine drug screen, meconium toxicology, etc.) requiring **cross-patient** linkage (mother→infant), which cannot be expressed in single-patient FHIR REST queries |
| **Methadone / buprenorphine** | Per PheKB, these are secondary treatment agents; expand MedicationRequest with RxNorm `6813` (methadone) and `30101` (buprenorphine) if broader pharmacologic sweep is desired |
